title: salix lagopina
intro: Salix lagopina, also known as Arctic willow or dwarf willow, is a small shrub native to the Arctic tundra in northern regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. It is a deciduous plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and bark of the Salix lagopina contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been used to treat fever, pain, and inflammation, providing health benefits such as reducing swelling and alleviating headaches. Salix lagopina can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the dried leaves and bark often used in herbal remedies to promote relaxation and soothe digestive issues.
